title: Highly Customizable with Stellar Support Team
reviewText:
I appreciate how 11x has helped me scale personalization on a more one-to-one level, using company and individual information effectively. I really like the AI-generated emails feature the most because it's tailored to my tone and able to pull in specific instructions and background research for personalized outreach. The website visitors feature is great because it helps me stay on top of mind with inbound traffic and understand which customers are resonating with our content. Additionally, I found the support team to be amazing, particularly Nick, who was instrumental in enabling my experience. He was always on point with creating campaigns and testing new ideas, which made my experience with 11x pleasant.
One thing that could be improved is 11x's ability to read responses, like on LinkedIn. It doesn't always follow up appropriately, even when someone has already replied. It sometimes sends the next message in the sequence that doesn’t fit the conversation anymore because the prospect has already responded. Also, 11x doesn't have a cold calling tool at the moment, which is something I know is on the horizon.
11x helps me scale personalization on a one-to-one level using company and individual info. It automates message creation with various tonalities for personalized outreach. The AI-generated emails tailor outreach based on my tone and instructions. Highly Customizable with Stellar Support Team

title: Efficient Lead Generation with Superb Support
reviewText:

I'm impressed with the quality of leads, which are generally good despite how niche some of our ICPs can be. The initial setup of 11x was super easy, and our integration with HubSpot works smoothly for the most part. Slack also works as advertised for notifications related to 11x warm leads.
Sometimes 11x misses the mark on an email or LinkedIn message sequence, whether it be not relevant to the target or too wordy.
I use 11x for lead generation, finding success primarily with LinkedIn connections. The tool's sleek interface, great customer support, and quality leads make it efficient. Issues can arise with message sequences needing refinement, but overall, it aids in reaching niche ICPs effectively. Efficient Lead Generation with Superb Support

title: 11x Makes Outreach and Campaign Creation Seamless and On-Brand
reviewText:
11x helps us streamline our outreach and cold outbounding in a way that's both efficient and effective. Creating new campaigns, targeting new audiences, and getting creative about new ways that we can connect with our target audience is made seamless with 11x. Not only is it easy to source new leads within our target market, it's easy to connect with them in a voice that is authentic to our product and our brand.
The most challenging part of the tool used to be proving value and ROI with the product, but 11x has already incorporated an influenced pipeline tool that automatically calculates this for users. I'd like the ability to refine this even further in platform, but 11x has done a great job building out this tool in a way that's responsive and thoughtful. 

The only other challenges I have with the tool would be the way that website visitors are tracked. I would love a way to filter out website visitors based on URL or pages visited. For us, one of our highest traffic pages is also end-user facing which makes it less valuable for us to market to, as most of those users are not within our target buyer demographic. Being able to filter this field would make difference in our use of the tool.
11x allows us to take a lot of manual cold outreach and even automated warm lead follow up off of our plate. 11x pulls its weight helping us source viable leads to provide our Sales team with more at bats. 

We're a very lean team and it's been important for us to leverage how easy campaign creation and target segmentation is through 11x. It's helped keep our BDR function running even amongst staffing changes and gaps. 11x Makes Outreach and Campaign Creation Seamless and On-Brand

title: Exceptional Service, Effortless Onboarding
reviewText:
I appreciate 11x for its outstanding ease of setup, thanks to the dedicated full-time account representative who was always available to assist us. This personal support made launching and configuring the software remarkably straightforward, allowing us to quickly get up and running by identifying targets and setting up processes effortlessly. Additionally, the AI agent named Alice is particularly impressive; the ability to nudge her in the right direction during campaigns is invaluable. Alice efficiently identifies targets and composes emails, and we can adjust our campaigns by adding or removing criteria to make our outreach more targeted. This flexibility to refine our messaging over time is a significant advantage. Beyond the technical features, the customer service from 11x is exceptional. The team, especially Nick and his boss Lauren, went above and beyond to ensure our success with the application, consistently offering their availability for immediate support through various communication channels like quick calls and Slack. This level of service made all the difference for us, making it the key reason to choose 11x despite other available options in the market.
I find the credit system in 11x to be a source of anxiety within my organization. Although the system is designed to control the bandwidth of outbound messages, there's a constant concern that we might consume more credits than we have. While customer support, notably our partner Nick, is quick to add credits if needed, the anxiety stemming from potential credit shortages persists despite knowing that support is readily available.
I find 11x scales our sales efforts by generating leads and identifying potential clients without additional headcount, optimizing email outreach and enhancing target identification. Exceptional Service, Effortless Onboarding

title: Finally, outbound that actually reaches the developers and AI builders we want to talk to
reviewText:
The signal-based targeting is what sets it apart for us. We sell an API to developers and AI teams, which is a notoriously hard audience to reach with cold outreach - they ignore generic emails instantly. With AiSDR we've been able to run campaigns targeting people actively engaging with specific tools in our space, referencing LinkedIn activity and public signals that are actually relevant to them. The messages don't feel like cold emails.
The CS support is amazing - Anna proactively answers any questions, suggests new campaign angles, and helps us with new features. It's more like having an outbound partner than a software subscription.
We've recently started using AI Monitoring, which has been a great addition on top of our Salesforce integration. It monitors our existing contact lists and triggers outreach at the right moment - when someone gets a new role, a company we're tracking raises funding, or a prospect signals a new initiative. For a technical audience that's hard to reach cold, being able to reach out with the right context at the right time makes a real difference.
We've had responses from senior AI engineers at major tech companies, which says a lot about the quality of targeting and personalization.
Persona customization can be tricky - it takes some iteration and the right inputs to get the right level of personalization. Also, the first month is slow by design (mailbox warm-up), so you need to plan for that ramp-up period before expecting real volume.
As a small team, we don't have the headcount to run structured outbound manually at scale. AiSDR lets us run multiple campaigns simultaneously (targeting different ICPs, geographies, and intent signals) without adding people. It's helped us reach developers and GTM leaders at companies we couldn't access through inbound alone.
The Salesforce integration keeps everything in sync without extra manual work, and with AI Monitoring layered on top. Finally, outbound that actually reaches the developers and AI builders we want to talk to

title: AiSDR Speeds Up Lead Follow-Up Without Adding Headcount
reviewText:
AiSDR has helped us move on demand faster and more consistently without adding a lot of manual overhead. It has created a much cleaner bridge between lead capture and actual follow-up, which is where things often break down. We’ve also been able to tailor the motion by campaign and source, and connect it into the rest of our stack, so it feels like part of our operating system rather than a standalone tool. The biggest upside is that it has helped us scale coverage and responsiveness without needing to scale headcount at the same rate.
Like most tools with a lot of flexibility, it can take a bit of iteration to get everything fully dialed in. The upside is that you can tailor workflows, messaging, and routing to match your motion, but the tradeoff is that it isn’t always a true set-it-and-forget-it product. I’d also love to see continued improvements to reporting and workflow visibility so it’s even easier to optimize and troubleshoot as things evolve. That said, for us the value has outweighed these limitations.
AiSDR is helping us close the gap between capturing demand and actually following up. Previously, there was too much risk that inbound interest or website activity wouldn’t be worked quickly or consistently enough. AiSDR has helped us create a more scalable process for routing and follow-up, while also enabling strong personalization and messaging so our outreach feels relevant rather than generic. As a result, we’re seeing faster response times, better coverage, and a more efficient way to turn interest into real conversations. AiSDR Speeds Up Lead Follow-Up Without Adding Headcount

title: Intuitive, Time-Saving Outreach with Seamless HubSpot & LinkedIn Integration
reviewText:

But with this easy to use program I can implement and get it going in under an hour.  And then the way we kicked off was also very easy and simple to follow.  The longest part was for the emails to get "warmed up" (they will explain it to you).  It was amazingly intuitive from the very beginning.  I think that was certainly helped by the fantastic and dedicated support team.  They are available at all kind of hours, and when they are not, the chatbot tool helps out.  While that one still needs a little work, it is definitely better than getting back radio silence.  I uses it everyday now without fail.  And the last bit which saved us tons of time was the way it integrated in seconds with our HubSpot and LinkedIn.  All in all, so far it has been a fantastic asset and force multiplier.
For me, being a relative old time with technology, it took some hand holding to get the terminology down and the overall process.  But I would not say it was completely aiSDR's fault, that was both of us.  Having said that, once I did understand the flow, it became easy to follow and use.  The other thing was the pricing model.  As of this writing there are only two choices, one relatively low and the other is nearly three times that.  They should have a middle tier then also a way just to buy more emails if you hit your limit.
The problem it is solving for us is how to get the most reach to the marketplace using the least amount of resources.  We are a a startup and must monitor our budget and balance it with the R&D spend.  With aiSDR I get a full team of sales people AND research assistants. title: AiSDR has been a game changer for hyper personalized outreach at scale
reviewText:
I've been using AiSDR for a bit over a month now and am very pleased with the platform, team support and results. Before starting, our outbound engine was struggling with email+LI and taking up a ton of effort+time. It's only been a month and we've already seen an increase in reply rates, decrease in bounce rates, and booked a handful of meetings. The team has been great to work with as they offer constant support and best practices.
Nothing I dislike at this point to be honest. For anyone looking to get started I will say three points of advice:
1) Treat the AI as if it's an actual SDR. It needs to be trained and ramped the same way a human would. The more effort you put into it's enablement early on, the more success you will have.
2) Give it time - I like that they offer month to month subscription so it's easy to get started, but don't expect to see tons of meetings booked in the first couple weeks. Again, consider it like a ramping human SDR in that sense.
3) While AiSDR is very autonomous, I still recommend monitoring it regularly to make sure it continues to target the right prospects and messaging.
Before AiSDR, we really struggled with email and LI outreach. Since starting with them we've been able to see a significant lift in results through multiple booked meetings, reply rates and decrease in bounce rates. AiSDR has been a game changer for hyper personalized outreach at scale

title: Clean, Intuitive Interface with Instant, Spot-On Sequence Generation
reviewText:
I was first introduced to Regie through a LinkedIn marketing group, and I was immediately impressed by its clean, intuitive interface. It feels very similar to other CRM tools I’ve used before, which made it easy to jump in and start inputting target data. The way the information is displayed is incredible. I clicked “generate” and was blown away to see a comprehensive 18-step sequence created almost instantly, with spot-on messaging for each touchpoint.
World tool is powerful. I’m still a bit on the learning curve when it comes to the Persona feature, and I haven’t quite mastered how to implement personas deeply into my sequences to make sure I’m hitting the exact right prospect profile every time. It’s not necessarily a flaw in the software, but I do think the onboarding around that specific targeting advantage could be a bit more guided.
It solves the massive headache of spending half a day manually drafting a sales cadence. What used to take me 3 to 5 hours of focused work is now finished in under 10 minutes. The direct integration with our CRM is the real winner: being able to import a fully formed sequence, with all touchpoints ready to go, saves me a huge amount of administrative work. Clean, Intuitive Interface with Instant, Spot-On Sequence Generation
switchedReason: We needed a more intelligent way to generate content, not just a place to host it. Our previous system was great for execution, but it didn’t help with the creative bottleneck of writing sequences. title: Great Automation, But Needs Better Quality Control
reviewText:
I found that Regie.ai does succeed in making work move at a quick pace. I was able to automate several repetitive tasks that usually take a lot of my time, like setting up the basic structure of a multi-touch campaign. It can certainly turn out a lot of material in a short amount of time, which is one saving grace.
Unfortunately, the quality isn't there for a professional environment. I found the persona to be very poor and highly inaccurate. The AI consistently failed to grasp the nuance of a specific target audience, leading to messaging that felt completely obvious. Further, the writing style was too salesy, like drafts produced by a generic hyperbolic style that modern consumers have learned to ignore. In today's world, sending out emails that sound like they were written by a 1990s telemarketer is actually counterproductive and risks damaging our brand reputation.
Regie.ai automates high-volume email campaigns, reducing manual labor. It's designed to handle bulk messaging without staffing more SDRs, aiming to clear lead backlogs and speed up processes. Great Automation, But Needs Better Quality Control

reviewText:

title: Apollo.io Mail Sequences Made Outreach Easy—and Brought in Leads
reviewText:

Problem: Sending personalized cold emails to 200 prospects every day, along with follow-up emails in a sequence, used to take far too much time. On top of that, when prospects were in different time zones, it became really difficult to send emails to each person at the right time. With Apollo io, there’s no hassle at all—it sends personalized emails based on their time zones, and that has benefited me. Apollo.io Mail Sequences Made Outreach Easy—and Brought in Leads

title: Everything I Needed for Outreach in One Place
reviewText:
The combination of a massive verified contact database and built-in sequencing is what keeps me coming back. Instead of juggling three different tools for finding leads, verifying emails, and sending follow-ups, Apollo handles it all in one place  that alone saves a couple of hours every week.

The filters are where it really shines. I can narrow down prospects by industry, company size, job title, and even tech stack, so I'm not wasting time on contacts that were never a good fit. Building a targeted list that used to take half a day now takes maybe 20 minutes.

The email sequencing with automatic follow-ups means I'm not manually tracking who I chased and when. I set the cadence once and Apollo keeps it moving, which has noticeably improved my reply rates just through consistency.

One thing I didn't expect: the Chrome extension is genuinely useful. I can pull up a LinkedIn profile and get contact details and company info without leaving the page, it's small but it adds up.

If you do any kind of cold outreach at scale, it's hard to justify cobbling together separate tools when Apollo does the job end to end.
The data accuracy is the biggest frustration. Email bounce rates are higher than I'd like. probably 10–15% of contacts have outdated information, which hurts deliverability if you're not scrubbing lists before sending. A more aggressive real-time verification pass before exporting would go a long way.

The interface can get cluttered once you're managing multiple sequences and lists simultaneously. Navigating between campaigns, contacts, and analytics feels like one click too many each time.

Credit limits are a genuine pain point. If you're doing heavy prospecting in a given week, you can burn through your monthly export credits faster than expected and then you're either pausing outreach or upgrading.

The reporting is decent but not deep enough. I can see open and reply rates, but drilling into which specific step in a sequence is causing drop-off takes more effort than it should. Better per-step analytics without needing to export data manually would make optimising sequences a lot easier.
Before Apollo, prospecting meant jumping between LinkedIn, Hunter.io, and a spreadsheet just to build one targeted list  then managing follow-ups manually, which meant leads regularly going cold simply because timing slipped.

We struggled with scattered tools and inconsistent follow-up, but now we can build a verified, filtered prospect list and launch a full sequence in under 30 minutes, which has resulted in more replies, fewer missed follow-ups, and roughly 60% less time spent on the prospecting process overall. Everything I Needed for Outreach in One Place

title: Accurate Data and Powerful Prospecting That Streamlines Lead Generation
reviewText:
What I like most about ZoomInfo Sales is its ability to provide accurate, up-to-date contact and company data, which makes prospecting and lead generation much more efficient. The platform’s advanced search and filtering capabilities help quickly identify the right decision-makers, while its integration with CRM systems keeps all sales data centralised and actionable. I also appreciate the insights and analytics it provides, which help prioritise high-value opportunities and improve targeting for outreach. Overall, it saves time, improves the quality of leads, and supports more data-driven sales decisions.
One downside of ZoomInfo Sales is that maintaining data accuracy can sometimes be a challenge, as contact details or company information can change quickly. The platform can also feel overwhelming at first due to the breadth of features and options available, which can require time to learn and optimise. Additionally, some of the more advanced functionalities may be limited to higher-tier plans, which can be a barrier for smaller teams or organisations. Overall, while highly useful, it requires some ongoing effort to get the most value from the tool.
ZoomInfo Sales solves the challenge of finding accurate and relevant prospect information quickly, which reduces time spent on research and improves the quality of outreach. It centralises company and contact data, allowing the sales team to target the right decision-makers with confidence. This leads to more efficient lead generation, higher-quality conversations, and better pipeline management. Overall, it saves time, increases productivity, and supports more data-driven sales strategies, helping the team close deals faster and with greater accuracy. Accurate Data and Powerful Prospecting That Streamlines Lead Generation

title: User-Friendly platform that helps you transform your business
reviewText:
I find ZoomInfo Sales very user-friendly and extremely easy to use. It’s dynamic and provides a lot of insight into potential prospects and decision-makers, even when full information isn’t available. We’ve run successful email marketing campaigns and closed good deals after using their contact data. The response rate for emails we’ve sent using ZoomInfo Sales contact information has been impressively good.

What I really like is that it helps us reach people we couldn’t have contacted directly through other tools or programs we’ve used in the past. The initial setup was straightforward, and the platform feels intuitive, which minimizes the need for extensive training.

Another function I’ve found especially useful is the company descriptions; the results we’ve gotten from them have been very accurate.
I don’t dislike anything about the tool. My main suggestion is to keep expanding the data coverage for Latin America. We also use it for searches in Europe, Asia, the Middle East, and the US, and in those regions I’ve found it to be very robust.

It would be great to improve the presence of, and access to, information for countries in Latin America. Also, some of the contact information isn’t updated—there are cases where contacts no longer work at their companies or don’t hold those positions anymore. That said, I understand this isn’t easy to keep current in a world where professional mobility moves so fast.

Overall, I love ZoomInfo and have already recommended it to former colleagues and family.
ZoomInfo Sales helps me find prospects and decision-makers worldwide, so I can reach people I can’t easily access directly or through other tools. It also strengthens our email campaigns and has helped us generate good deals. User-Friendly platform that helps you transform your business

title: Comprehensive Data, Phenomenal Support & Easy/Precise Filter Methods for Accurate Target Audience
reviewText:
I absolutely love ZoomInfo Sales. I've recommended it to a bunch of friends and helped others within their organizations learn how to use it better. The intent and filtering features are incredibly useful and important to me, especially when it comes to revenue amounts and locations. My account manager has been really good, showing me the ropes and being available when needed. ZoomInfo Sales makes it easy to find the correct decision maker by allowing me to input multiple job titles, which is super helpful when dealing with thousands of contacts. I love being able to find cell phone numbers and email addresses, and there’s a lot of valuable information available about the companies, including what they're searching for, which is part of the intent that I really like. My favorite thing is being able to do searches and create qualified lists with filters like revenue, location, and industry, helping me reach the right people. The interface works really well, and I appreciate the speedy communication and resolutions I receive. It was really easy to integrate, I use it 50-100 times a day, & I'm SO glad we have it!!
Sometimes, if a person's name hasn't been used frequently or searched for in the past ninety days, it doesn't show up in the immediate search with the criteria I put in. Even if I do type somebody's name in very specifically, then it'll pop up, which is a bit of a challenge. It's kind of a turnoff when I'm trying to seek out a company and see only 10 employees, making me think I've reached the end of my search. I wish they wouldn't quite be filtered out like that.
I find ZoomInfo Sales incredibly useful for identifying decision-makers, filtering by job title and location, and accessing vital company information. title: Efficient Enrichment with Minor Hiccups
reviewText:

The Salesforce sync is another feature I find valuable, as it operates reliably and ensures our data is accurately updated without needing constant oversight.
The credit model could be better. Failed enrichments still burn credits, and we've blown through monthly credits early a couple of times. Some kind of cost estimate would be a good solution. The UI looks like a friendly spreadsheet, but when you need conditional logic and formula chains, it's basically programming. The phone number coverage is really weak, with mobile matches only around 30-35%. Support could also be quicker; the back and forth on tickets takes longer, especially when something breaks mid-campaign.

reviewText:

title: Powerful automation that makes outbound sales much faster and easier
reviewText:
I use Clay to handle the complicated research and lead enrichment that other tools can’t do. What I like most is the “waterfall” enrichment feature: it lets me pull data from multiple sources at once, so if one provider doesn’t have an email, Clay automatically finds it through another source. The UI feels like a smart spreadsheet, which makes it easy to organize my work without needing to be a programmer. I also love Claygent, the AI agent, because it can visit company websites to find specific news or signals that I used to search for manually. That added intelligence has helped me build better GTM workflows and onboard new reps much faster.
The biggest drawback for me is the learning curve. It’s clearly a very powerful tool, but it takes time to figure out how to build the most effective workflows and write prompts that work well with the AI. I also find the credit-based pricing a little difficult to predict. If you run a large list with too many enrichment steps, credits can disappear quickly, so you really have to plan your tasks carefully and keep an eye on what you’re running. On top of that, performance can sometimes feel a bit slow when processing thousands of rows at once, which definitely requires some patience.
Clay solved the problem of manual, slow research for my outbound sales campaigns. Before using Clay, I spent hours every day checking LinkedIn and company websites to find the right information for my leads. With Clay, I can research at scale instead of doing everything one lead at a time. Now I have an automated system that enriches and scores my leads in minutes, which has been a huge benefit. Overall, it reduced my research time by 60–70%. Powerful automation that makes outbound sales much faster and easier

title: Centralizes and Automates Prospect Research Efficiently
reviewText:
I use Clay mainly as a sales intelligence and contact enrichment tool. I like how it centralizes and automates nearly all my prospect research and contact enrichment in one place, saving me an enormous amount of time. Instead of jumping between LinkedIn, company websites, and our CRM, I can get clean, up-to-date profiles for prospects and decision-makers almost instantly. I appreciate how powerful yet intuitive the integrations are, especially with LinkedIn, email tools, and our CRM. The ability to pull in data, enrich records, and export directly to my workflow tools makes outreach much more efficient. For B2B sales and business development in Germany, where accurate, well-researched outreach is important, this consistency is a huge advantage. Clay acts as a hub for cleaner, richer, more actionable B2B contact data that supports smarter outreach and stronger client relationships.
User management and team permissions lack granularity. We work with multiple teams and regional offices in Germany, and we need tight control over who can access certain datasets, export information, or modify workflows. But the current settings are fairly limited — it’s hard to restrict access to specific lists or prevent accidental changes to shared automations without complicated workarounds.
I use Clay to streamline sales intelligence and contact enrichment, quickly finding and organizing accurate data, centralizing everything for easy access. This boosts my outreach efficiency. title: It's extremely useful and flexible but be prepared for the learning curve
reviewText:
Clay is incredibly powerful as a data enrichment and workflow tool. I like how flexible it is—you can connect it with other platforms, build custom flows, and really tailor it to your specific use cases. The enrichment features are especially helpful for organizing and qualifying contacts, and the ability to automate pieces of outreach saves a lot of time. Once you understand its potential, it feels like a Swiss Army knife for GTM and operations.
The same flexibility that makes Clay powerful can also make it intimidating at first. There’s definitely a learning curve, especially if you’re not familiar with enrichment logic or building workflows. Some features aren’t always intuitive, and it takes experimentation to unlock its full value. That said, once you invest the time, the payoff is significant.
Clay is like Notion but for Data—it centralizes enrichment, sales intelligence, and lead intelligence in one place. Instead of juggling multiple tools and spreadsheets, I can use Clay to collect, clean, and enrich data, then push it directly into workflows. It saves time by automating repetitive research tasks and makes it easier to keep my lead and sales intelligence organized and actionable. It feels almost like “what can’t Clay do?” because of how flexible it is across use cases, but the biggest benefit is having one platform where my data is both accessible and operational. It's extremely useful and flexible but be prepared for the learning curve

title: Clay is our go-to tool for generating results for B2B companies!
reviewText:
Clay is hands-down the most powerful data enrichment and outreach automation platform I've used. Its modular, spreadsheet-like interface is intuitive yet incredibly powerful. 

With native integrations to tools like Apollo, Clearbit, Google Maps, LinkedIn, and built-in AI steps, it allows you to build complex workflows without writing a line of code. 

You can create highly personalized outbound campaigns, enrich data at scale, scrape websites, validate emails, and trigger sequences—all in one place.
The learning curve can be steep at first. While the UI is elegant, understanding how to structure your data and optimize performance takes some trial and error. 

Also, some APIs have rate limits or costs that need to be monitored, especially when scaling to large datasets.

However, the Clay team is very responsive to all feedback.
Clay has completely changed how we do outbound sales and lead research. Instead of manually sourcing and enriching leads, we now automate the entire process - from ICP definition to campaign-ready lists. It's also helped us reduce costs significantly by caching results and avoiding redundant API calls. 

For agencies like ours, it’s a backend engine that powers dozens of campaigns without the need for a large team. Clay is our go-to tool for generating results for B2B companies!
